taskCaption: cube starts AWAY from the tray (FAR) and is placed inside it (placed-from-outside diagnostic)

pilotMetadata.tests: Sibling of relation_event for real put-ins that start FAR (cube placed from across the table). Asserts via the FROZEN matcher: terminal INSIDE (goal_satisfaction); the cube STARTED FAR_FROM, not already inside (initial_state, from the authored r0 FAR_FROM endpoint); ended INSIDE (terminal_state + relation_transitions); one CONTAINMENT_CHANGE present (event_presence). Real successes split ~2:1 FAR:NEAR at the start, so relation_event (initial NEAR) alone false-negatives the far-start put-ins; this target recovers them. A born-inside cube (initial INSIDE) FAILs initial_state here exactly as in relation_event. Combined put-in verdict = relation_event PASS OR placed_from_outside PASS; born-inside FAILs both.
addedOverTerminalOnly: relations(authored NEAR r0 + INSIDE r1) -> contributes initial_state(NEAR) + terminal_state(INSIDE)
pilotMetadata.enforcedVsAuthoring: CRITICAL: a 'born-inside' episode is rejected by initial_state, NOT by relation_transitions/event_presence. csg/rollout_extract.py seeds prev_rel='NEAR' UNCONDITIONALLY, so a cube that ends INSIDE -- even one inside the whole time -- ALWAYS emits a NEAR->INSIDE CONTAINMENT_CHANGE delta. The only thing distinguishing a real put-in from born-inside is the robot's FIRST relation fact (NEAR for a real put-in, INSIDE for born-inside), caught by initial_state. ENFORCED: terminal INSIDE, INSIDE achieved (relation_transitions), initial NEAR endpoint (initial_state), CONTAINMENT_CHANGE present (event_presence). event_order stays support 0 (one event, no pair to order against). AUTHORING ONLY (not verifier-enforced): timeSpan magnitudes, confidences, geometry source/category.
pilotMetadata.deferredNote: Marker-only 3A has no honest evidence for who/what caused the motion; asserting contact or causal order would be unevidenced. Those remain a later contact/order target.
pilotMetadata.notAGoldTask: Pilot diagnostic, deliberately NOT added to gold_tests/. It asserts the cube started near and was placed inside the tray; it does NOT assert the agent contacted or caused the motion. Read it for relation-event mappability, not as a full object_inside_container acceptance test.
pilotMetadata.whyNotJustRelaxRelationEvent: The frozen matcher's initial_state is directional-missing over AUTHORED facts; it cannot express 'initial NEAR-or-FAR' (an OR) in one target, so we keep both and OR them at the pilot. csg/ stays byte-frozen.
